# djlib-doctor Full Model Context

`djlib-doctor` is an open-source DJ library verification, cleanup planning, staging, and migration toolkit under `pseudovirtual/djlib-doctor`.

## Purpose

Help DJs inspect, clean up, and migrate local Rekordbox and Serato libraries without losing cue work, playlist intent, or trust in their files.

The project is read-only-first. Write-capable workflows are explicit, staged, token-gated, backed up, and verified.

## Implemented

- Rekordbox XML parsing for collection tracks, playlist references, memory cues, hotcues, and loops
- local file, streaming placeholder, and unknown-location classification
- verification reports, snapshots, redacted snapshots, and schema discovery
- missing-file, duplicate, cue-coverage, bad-path, and audio compatibility plans
- duplicate collision policies: `cue-safe`, `quality`, and `keep-both`
- interactive CLI review, decision sheets, and dry-run apply manifests
- baseline/final Rekordbox export comparison
- read-only Serato `root.sqlite` inspection
- Rekordbox-to-Serato port manifests, crate previews, batch playlist planning, and summary-only checks
- Serato-to-Rekordbox port manifests and Rekordbox XML preview representations
- scoped migration by track, playlist/crate, multi-playlist, or collection
- `full`, `cues-only`, and `match-only` transfer modes
- staged Serato SQLite/crate installs with backups, sidecar checks, app-closed checks, exact tokens, and hash verification
- staged Serato audio tag writes for AIFF/AIF, M4A/MP4, and MP3
- staged Rekordbox DB imports from Serato-to-Rekordbox port manifests through copied `master.db` files
- lower-level staged Rekordbox SQLite operations for reviewed operation manifests
- staged file copy, move, delete, and ffmpeg conversion operations
- Codex `AGENTS.md`, Claude `CLAUDE.md`, llms files, repo-scoped skill, plugin skeleton, and Claude Desktop extension template

## Not Implemented

- standalone MCP server
- repository-side PyPI trusted publishing configuration
- broad real-world Rekordbox DB schema adapter coverage

## Safety

Do not write to live databases, music files, or Serato audio tags from planning commands. Writes are allowed only through staged install/apply commands with exact confirmation tokens:

- `install serato-stage`
- `install serato-tags`
- `install rekordbox-db`
- `install file-ops`

For Serato-to-Rekordbox writes, use:

```bash
djlib-doctor port serato-to-rb --serato-library-dir "/path/to/serato-library" --crate "/path/to/_Serato_/Subcrates/My.crate" --collection-root ~/Music --out run/serato-to-rb
djlib-doctor stage rekordbox-db-import --db /path/to/rekordbox/master.db --port-manifest run/serato-to-rb/port-manifest.json --stage-dir run/rekordbox-stage
djlib-doctor install rekordbox-db --stage-dir run/rekordbox-stage --db /path/to/rekordbox/master.db --confirm-token INSTALL_SQLITE_STAGE:...
```

Or use the workflow shortcut:

```bash
djlib-doctor migrate serato-to-rb --serato-library-dir "/path/to/serato-library" --crate "/path/to/_Serato_/Subcrates/My.crate" --collection-root ~/Music --out run/serato-to-rb --stage-db --rekordbox-db /path/to/rekordbox/master.db
```

The Rekordbox DB importer fails closed if the target schema is unsupported.

## Good Agent Workflow

1. Read `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, or `.agents/skills/djlib-doctor/SKILL.md`.
2. Run `verify`, `snapshot`, `inspect`, or `port` before any staging command.
3. Explain findings in DJ language.
4. Ask the user to choose scope and transfer mode.
5. Use `review` for row-by-row human decisions.
6. Inspect generated manifests before staging.
7. Install only when the user supplies the exact token and confirms the relevant app is closed.
